message.content: Adversaries may exfiltrate data by transferring it to another cloud account they control within the same service, circumventing traditional detection methods focused on external file transfers or network-based exfiltration. This technique leverages existing cloud provider APIs and internal address spaces, making the data transfer appear as normal traffic and avoiding external network interfaces. Common exploitation methods include creating backups of cloud instances and transferring them to separate accounts. The potential impact includes unauthorized access to sensitive data and significant data breaches. Mitigation strategies involve monitoring for unusual account activity within the cloud environment, implementing strict access controls, and using anomaly detection to identify atypical data transfers between accounts. Regular audits and logging of cloud account activities can also help in early detection and response.
